summary: A rectangular, two-story fortress-like building with thick corner towers and an open central courtyard. The exterior walls are banded with stone and light-colored horizontal trim, small slit windows run along each wall, and four tall dark stone towers rise at the corners with wide overhanging flat roof caps. The courtyard contains a raised central platform and a regular grid of short white posts or lights along a grass floor.

shape.overall_form: rectangular block surrounding an open central courtyard with corner towers
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along both axes (appears roughly four-fold around courtyard)

materials.name: brick
materials.role: darker textured corner towers (looks like cracked/regular variant)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: horizontal accent strips and tower roof tops (planks)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: flat roof caps and horizontal ledges

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: thin wooden trim under roof caps (possible slabs)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: interior courtyard posts or decorative short pillars (may be white blocks)

elements: corner towers (4) with overhanging flat caps
two-story continuous exterior walls with horizontal trim bands
rows of small glass windows/slits on each wall
open central courtyard with grass and central raised platform
short white block perimeter decorative posts at ground level
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular outer shell forming a hollow courtyard; exterior wall height ~10–12 blocks with internal courtyard open to sky.

Place four tall corner towers rising above the walls; towers are square columns ~3–4 blocks wide and extend several blocks higher than the walls, each topped by a layered flat cap: stone slab outer rim, wooden slab/strip underlayer and a center beacon-like light.

Exterior walls use horizontal banding: darker stone mid-band, lighter trimmed layers above and below, with a wood plank stripe between stone bands. Repeat small 1x2 glass window slits in regular rows across each long face.

Courtyard: grass floor with diagonal pattern of short white posts (approx. 1 block high) and a central raised square platform of stone slabs; surround the courtyard edge with a low white coping and an inner ledge.
Add white square decorative blocks along the outside base evenly spaced (approx. 1 block each) to match visible perimeter stones.
uncertainties: Exact block types for dark corner towers: looks like stonebrick variants (could be cracked stonebrick or another dark masonry) — identified as 'brick' with medium confidence.
Some white posts/trim may be quartz or snow or white concrete; material normalized to generic white/fence/slab options — exact block uncertain.
Precise internal floor layout and access points between courtyard and interior are not visible; assumed open courtyard with perimeter walkways.
Exact dimensions (width/depth/height) are estimated from visible block counts in images and may be off by several blocks.
